channel.title: Metals Industry News Feed
channel.description: Automated news feed for aluminum, steel, copper, and nickel industries

item.title: AI data centers' massive demand for aluminum is crushing the US aluminum industry
item.description: A new aluminum smelter in Oklahoma with a capacity of about 600,000 tons per year is nearly doubling current US domestic production, yet only six smelting operations remain with just four actively producing. Even at full capacity, US smelters could only satisfy about a third of domestic demand, requiring five years and $25 billion investment to meet current needs.

item.title: Copper futures price today
item.description: Investing.com shows copper futures trading at 6.3370, down from a previous close of 6.3855. The page provides live pricing and recent market information, making it useful for tracking near-term copper moves.

item.title: Copper Jul 26 (HG=F) stock price, news, quote & history
item.description: Yahoo Finance lists copper July 2026 futures at 6.385, with an intraday range of 6.270 to 6.385. The page also aggregates recent news and market data relevant to copper price trends.

item.title: Copper market news & forecasts
item.description: Fastmarkets says copper’s long-term outlook remains constrained by the industry’s limited ability to bring new supply online quickly enough to meet rising demand. The publication highlights permitting delays and supply constraints as key themes shaping the market.

item.title: Aosta, Cogne Acciai Speciali: bilancio in rosso e futuro incerto
item.description: The article reports growing concern over Cogne Acciai Speciali’s future in Aosta after a sharp deterioration in its financial results. It says the company recorded a loss of 60.9 million euros in 2025, down from an 8 million euro profit the year before, amid U.S. tariffs, Asian competition, and higher energy costs.

item.title: Torna la cassa integrazione in Cogne Acciai Speciali
item.description: This piece reports that wage-support measures are returning at Cogne Acciai Speciali, affecting 750 employees for 13 weeks from June 9 to September 7. It frames the move as part of a difficult industrial situation for the company.

item.title: Indonesia navigates nickel market with output cuts, policy shifts
item.description: Indonesia confirmed plans to cut nickel production in 2026 to support prices and government revenue while cracking down on environmentally harmful mining operations. The country is reversing its mining quota policy to regulate supply volatility as actual production continues to lag behind targets.

item.title: Nickel price spikes as risks to supply grow in top producer Indonesia
item.description: Nickel futures rallied sharply on the London Metal Exchange after reports that 10% to 15% of high-grade nickel pig iron capacity at Indonesia's Weda Bay Industrial Park will undergo rotational maintenance. This supply risk in the world's top nickel producer has intensified market concerns about future availability.

item.title: Aluminum rose to 3,402.48 USD/T on June 22, 2026
item.description: Aluminum prices were slightly higher in the latest market update, rising to 3,402.48 USD/T, a gain of 0.05% from the previous day. The data also shows the commodity has fallen 7.50% over the past month, despite remaining far above year-ago levels.

item.title: Aluminum Futures, Sep-2026 (ALI=F)
item.description: Yahoo Finance shows aluminum futures trading at 3,475.25, up 2.71% at the time of the quote. The market page suggests a stronger short-term move in futures compared with other commodity price trackers in the same period.

item.title: Copper futures hold near recent levels as market watches supply and demand signals
item.description: Copper futures were reported around $6.3 per pound on Monday, extending recent losses. The update attributed price pressure to resumed exports of copper concentrate from Rio Tinto's Oyu Tolgoi operations, alongside broader market attention on supply developments.

item.title: Copper prices surge on geopolitical tensions, supply shortages, and AI demand
item.description: The article says copper prices are up about 9% since the Iran war and are approaching a January peak. It highlights a mix of geopolitical risk, structural supply shortages, and AI-driven demand as key forces supporting the market.

item.title: Copper market outlook remains tight amid limited new supply
item.description: Fastmarkets says copper's long-term outlook is constrained by the industry's limited ability to bring new supply online quickly enough to meet demand. It points to permitting delays and broader supply-side bottlenecks as persistent market themes.

item.title: Nickel rose to 17,840.25 USD/T on June 22, 2026
item.description: Trading Economics reports that nickel gained 1.74% on June 22, 2026, indicating a firmer market in the latest session. It also notes that the metal is still down over the past month, showing recent volatility despite the day’s rebound.

item.title: Nickel Futures Price Today
item.description: Investing.com shows nickel futures trading around 17,550 to 17,701, with an intraday range between 17,489.50 and 17,765. The data suggests a relatively tight trading band in the latest session.

item.title: Steel Market Update: Home
item.description: Nucor announced on Monday, June 22, that its consumer spot price (CSP) for hot-rolled coil will be $1,130 per short ton for the week, an increase of $5/st from last week. This update reflects the latest spot pricing trends in the steel market.

item.title: 3 technologies helping the aluminum industry decarbonize
item.description: This article highlights Hydro's HalZero technology, which eliminates CO2 emissions during aluminum smelting by keeping carbon and chlorine in a closed loop. It also covers innovations in removing metal impurities from recycled aluminum and Rio Tinto's use of hydrogen to replace natural gas in alumina refining.

item.title: Aluminium Spot Price Chart | Live Price of Aluminium per Ounce
item.description: Aluminum spot prices fell to $3,251.22 per ton on June 23, 2026, representing a 4.67% decline. The live price data highlights a significant drop in aluminum values, with the market showing continued weakness.

item.title: US Steel Costs Impacted by War, Trade Deals
item.description: Steel prices have continued to rise despite global conflicts, with hot-rolled coil reaching approximately $1,100 per ton. The typical steel pricing upcycle lasts 3–4 months, but the current nine-month upward trend suggests further increases, potentially reaching $1,500 per ton.

item.title: Steel Market Update: US sheet prices continue to move higher
item.description: US sheet steel prices are rising due to extremely limited spot availability, solid demand, and extended lead times. The market remains tight as suppliers struggle to meet growing customer orders.

item.title: U.S. Steel Manufacturers Plan Near-Term Capacity Additions
item.description: Steel manufacturers across the U.S. are planning over $6 billion in capital-spending projects set to begin in the second half of 2026, signaling near-term capacity growth. These investments aim to strengthen domestic production capabilities amid recovering demand and market stabilization.

item.title: Steel Production in the United States increased to 7500 Thousand Tonnes in May from 7200 Thousand Tonnes in April of 2026
item.description: U.S. steel production rose to 7.5 million tonnes in May 2026, up from 7.2 million tonnes in April, according to Trading Economics. The increase reflects a positive trend in domestic output, supported by recovering demand and improved mill utilization rates.

item.title: Total raw steel produced around the globe rebounded 3% in May to an estimated 157.9 million metric tons
item.description: Global raw steel production increased by 3% in May 2026, reaching an estimated 157.9 million metric tons, as reported by the World Steel Association. This rebound suggests a recovery in worldwide steel output amid stabilizing market conditions and rising demand.

item.title: Copper for Tomorrow: Sustainable copper production
item.description: The 'Copper for Tomorrow' initiative aims to solve value chain challenges to increase sustainable copper production globally. This bid focuses on integrating circular economy principles and digital advancements to lead toward a greener future.

item.title: Steel Price News: Are Prices About to Drop? Latest Hot Rolled Coil
item.description: Current hot-rolled coil prices stand at $1,109 per ton, with cold-rolled steel at $1,293 and coated steel at $1,380 per ton. Market analysts are evaluating whether these elevated prices will soon decline amid shifting supply dynamics.

item.title: HRC Steel fell to 1,193.04 USD/T on June 24, 2026
item.description: HRC Steel prices dropped slightly by 0.08% to $1,193.04 per ton on June 24, 2026, though they remain 1.88% higher over the past month and 34.20% higher than a year ago. The market shows resilience despite minor daily fluctuations.

item.title: Steel rebar futures fell below CNY 3,070 per ton
item.description: Steel rebar futures declined below CNY 3,070 per ton, with prices falling 3.51% over the past month but still 4.41% higher than a year ago. This indicates a short-term dip within a longer-term upward trend.

item.title: Nickel – a mineral with a challenging role in clean tech
item.description: Nickel is crucial for the net-zero transition with demand expected to grow 65% this decade, making it imperative that production scales up with minimal environmental impact. A meta-analysis confirms that clean energy technologies are the fastest-growing sector for nickel demand, requiring the industry to adopt more sustainable practices.

item.title: Tenaris acquires control of Italy-based Fast
item.description: Luxembourg-based global steel pipe manufacturer Tenaris has announced the acquisition of control of Fast Srl, a Bergamo-based company that manufactures equipment and products for oil and gas extraction. This strategic move strengthens Tenaris's presence in the Italian industrial sector.

item.title: Italy's Prysmian sees stronger U.S. pricing, margins after new copper tariffs
item.description: Milan-listed Prysmian, the world's largest cable maker, reported a 2.1% share increase following announcements of stronger U.S. pricing and margins due to new copper tariffs. Analysts at Citi suggest these tariffs will support pricing as the company benefits from domestic copper procurement amid U.S. tax adjustments on imported cables[1].

item.title: Major US aluminum producer boosts production to full capacity
item.description: Century Aluminum has decided to boost production to full capacity amid President Donald Trump's tariffs on aluminum. The move reflects growing confidence in the U.S. aluminum market under new trade policies.

item.title: America's big new aluminum smelter is still waiting on a power deal
item.description: A new energy-hungry aluminum smelter, set to be America's first since 1980, is awaiting a final power agreement. Once operational, it will more than double the nation's primary aluminum production capacity.

item.title: The Boom in New Steel Mills Is Outpacing Demand
item.description: Twenty-one million tons of additional steel production capacity has been announced or placed under construction in the past four years, signaling the most significant expansion of U.S. steelmaking capabilities in recent history. However, this growth risks outpacing demand as manufacturers build new facilities to meet domestic market needs.

item.title: Copper Holds Decline as Dollar Strengthens
item.description: Copper futures traded around $6.1 per pound on Wednesday after tumbling more than 3% in the previous session. The decline was driven by a strengthening U.S. dollar, which increased pressure on commodity prices.

item.title: Nickel Below $17K: Macro Pressure, Not Fundamental Weakness
item.description: Nickel prices have slipped below $17,000 per tonne in June 2026, down from the $18,500–$20,000 range seen earlier. The decline is attributed to macroeconomic pressure rather than fundamental weakness in the nickel market.

item.title: Aluminum hits four-year high on Mideast supply loss
item.description: This video report explains that aluminum prices reached a four-year high due to supply deficits caused by attacks in the Middle East, with an estimated 3 million tons shortfall in 2026. The segment projects substantial deficits for the current year and next, as declines in the Middle East cannot be offset by other sources.

item.title: Aluminum Prices Have Surged Nearly 90% Since the Iran War Began
item.description: Aluminum prices have surged nearly 90% since the Iran War began, prompting Kiplinger to advise investors to adjust their portfolios accordingly. The significant price move has been a key focus for investors tracking commodity trends.

item.title: Aluminum Market Trends
item.description: Extruders remain cautious for 2026 following an up-and-down 2025, with trade uncertainty and supply tightness pushing the Midwest Premium above $1/lb. The market is navigating ongoing challenges in supply and demand dynamics.

item.title: Steel fell to 3,078 CNY/T on June 25, 2026, down 0.13% from the previous day
item.description: Steel prices dropped slightly to 3,078 CNY per ton on June 25, 2026, marking a 0.13% decline from the prior day. Over the past month, steel prices have fallen 2.56%, though they remain 4.37% higher than the same period last year[5].

item.title: Steel Market Prices, Data, and Analysis | S&P Global
item.description: S&P Global provides current steel prices and market data updated 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, offering historical and spot price insights for ferrous metals[8].

item.title: Tenaris acquires control of Italy-based Fast Srl
item.description: Luxembourg-based global steel pipe manufacturer Tenaris has announced the acquisition of control of Fast Srl, a Bergamo-based company that manufactures equipment and products for oil and gas extraction. This strategic move strengthens Tenaris's presence in the Italian industrial sector.

item.title: Aluminum Rally Builds as War Risks Threaten Global Supply Chains
item.description: StoneX explains how Middle East disruptions, constrained global production, and China's capacity limits are reshaping the aluminum market, with China approaching its self-imposed 45.5 million ton cap. The analysis suggests China will likely reach this capacity limit within 2026, intensifying global supply concerns.

item.title: Steel Price Rose to 3067 CNY/T on June 26, 2026
item.description: Steel prices increased to 3067 CNY per ton on June 26, 2026, marking a 0.29% rise from the previous day. Although the price has fallen 2.91% over the past month, it remains 4.00% higher than the same period last year.

item.title: Flat-Rolled Steel Prices Update: CR at 1,320–1,360
item.description: Steel Market Update provides current flat-rolled steel prices, including Cold Rolled (CR) at 1,320–1,360, Galvanized (GI) at 1,300–1,340, and Aluminum-Zinc (AZ) at 1,300–1,350. These figures reflect recent market fluctuations and pricing trends for flat-rolled steel products.

item.title: Steel Production Dips in the U.S. | #ScrapMonsterNews
item.description: The latest data from AISI shows a 2.4% dip in weekly U.S. raw steel production, with the Southern region still facing challenges. This decline marks a softening in U.S. steel output as reported by ScrapMonsterNews.

item.title: Steel imports down 30% in 2026 as tariffs bolster US production
item.description: Foreign steel entering the United States remains about 30% lower on a year-to-date basis as Section 232 tariffs continue to disrupt trade flows and support domestic steel production. Despite a 6% monthly increase in April, tariffs have significantly reduced overall imports.

item.title: Global steel industry battles surplus supply, protectionism
item.description: Steelmaking capacity is expected to rise for a seventh consecutive year, reaching 2.55 billion metric tons by the end of 2025, according to the OECD steel report. The report pegged 2025 surplus capacity at 680 million mt, with China contending with poor domestic demand and minimal capacity reduction efforts.

item.title: Nickel fell to $16,700 per tonne, nearing its lowest level since late December
item.description: Nickel fell to $16,700 per tonne, nearing its lowest level since late December, as Indonesia prepares to permit a substantial increase in nickel mine output. This potential supply surge is contributing to the downward pressure on prices.

item.title: Prysmian plans New York listing and more acquisitions in US
item.description: The Italian cable company Prysmian is planning a New York stock listing and further acquisitions in the United States to insulate itself from potential trade tensions. The company aims to establish a manufacturing base in America to reduce exposure to tariffs and regulatory risks.

item.title: Prysmian receives the notice to proceed for the Italy-Tunisia electrical interconnector
item.description: Prysmian has been officially notified to proceed with the construction of the Italy-Tunisia electrical interconnector, a major energy infrastructure project. This development underscores Prysmian's role in advancing cross-border energy connectivity between Europe and Africa.

item.title: Aluminum Prices Are Giving Up Some Gains Despite Gulf Disruptions
item.description: In early U.S. trading on Thursday, aluminum prices retreated 6% to $3,214.50, despite ongoing disruptions in the Middle East jeopardizing supply chains. A reduction in demand coupled with increasing inventories in China is moderating some of the previous price increases.

item.title: Steel rose to 3064 CNY/T on June 26, 2026
item.description: Steel prices rose to 3064 CNY/T on June 26, 2026, marking a 0.20% increase from the previous day. Over the past month, steel prices have fallen 2.95%, though they remain 3.44% higher than the previous year.

item.title: 3 innovations in green steel
item.description: Boston Metal, a Bill Gates-backed startup, raised $50 million to develop a CO2-free steel process using molten oxide electrolysis. Sweden is investing $3.99 billion to build a commercial hydrogen steel plant, while Kobe Steel introduced a new technique for steel production in natural gas blast furnaces that reduces coke usage.

item.title: Smelting Barriers: The Innovators Forging a New Future for Iron and Steel
item.description: Northern Sweden will produce the first commercial volumes of near-zero emissions steel, marking a breakthrough in direct electrification and material feedstock innovations. Companies like GreenOre are monetizing steel slag waste streams to create supplementary cementitious materials, reducing environmental pollution and accelerating commercial traction.

item.title: Green transformation in the iron and steel industry in India
item.description: New technologies are being developed in India that significantly cut carbon emissions in the iron and steel sector, with steel majors actively pursuing various technology avenues and seeking relevant partners. These innovations aim to make the industry more sustainable and economically viable while addressing climate goals.

item.title: Innovations in Heat & Water Recovery for a Greener Industry
item.description: The Heat Pipe Condensing Economiser (HPCE) technology is transforming the steel sector by improving heat and water recovery, making it more sustainable and economically viable. This innovation helps reduce energy consumption and emissions in industrial steel production processes.

item.title: The Future of Steel: Innovation and Sustainability with U. S. Steel
item.description: United States Steel Corporation unveils the pivotal role of steel in modern manufacturing and its contribution to sustainability through innovation. The video highlights how steel enables lighter, more fuel-efficient vehicles and taller, safer buildings while advancing green technology.

item.title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News - Trading Economics
item.description: Nickel fell to $16,700 per tonne on June 26, 2026, nearing its lowest level since late December[7]. The price dropped to 16,805 USD/T on June 26, 2026, down 0.36% from the previous day[7].

item.title: Can nickel prices hit $25,000 in 2026?
item.description: The Oregon Group projects nickel prices could reach $25,000 per tonne if supply tightens, noting that Indonesia's approved ore capacity was only 55% utilized in 2025, revealing a 'phantom surplus.' Indonesia has signaled major supply cuts for 2026, including banning new smelters and shortening quota validity to one year to manage pricing.

item.title: Aluminium price
item.description: On June 26, 2026, the aluminium price was recorded at 1.1399, equivalent to $3,176, with a monthly average of $3,469. The lowest value in the period was $3,146, indicating a slight downward trend from the monthly average.

item.title: Aluminum Prices Ease After Iran Shock as Trade Pressure Keeps Costs in Focus
item.description: Aluminum futures traded around $3,400 per ton, falling below peak-crisis forecasts that circulated earlier in the Iran-US conflict. The price decline reflects easing tensions following the shock, though trade pressure continues to keep costs in focus for the market.

item.title: Aluminum price falls on US-Iran deal but supply normalization to take time: analysts
item.description: Analysts report that aluminum prices dropped following the US-Iran deal, yet they caution that normalizing global supply will take time. The market is also seeing aluminum recycling accelerate as a shift from a green initiative to a necessity.

item.title: US aluminum, steel prices jump on Trump's new tariffs
item.description: US steel and aluminum prices soared after President Donald Trump announced he would double tariffs on the two metals to 50%, intensifying the global trade war. Foreign steelmaker shares slumped as the new tariffs are set to take effect on June 4th.

item.title: Copper prices are surging, driven by a confluence of factors including geopolitical tensions, structural supply shortages, and booming AI-driven demand
item.description: Copper prices are surging due to geopolitical tensions, structural supply shortages, and booming AI-driven demand. The metal is increasingly viewed as a strategic commodity for the AI era, with markets anticipating a prolonged supply crunch due to underinvestment and long lead times for new mining projects.

item.title: Copper prices reach a new record high after US announces copper tariffs aligned with existing 50% on steel, aluminium
item.description: Copper prices hit a record high after the US announced 50% tariffs on copper, aligned with existing tariffs on steel and aluminum. At one point, prices surged 17% to hit an all-time high of around $5.9 a barrel, with US copper trading at a record premium of $2,750 a ton.

item.title: Copper's long-term outlook is constrained by the industry's limited ability to bring new supply online fast enough to meet rising demand
item.description: Copper's long-term outlook is constrained by the industry's limited ability to bring new supply online quickly to meet rising demand, with permitting delays further complicating the situation. This supply crunch is expected to persist due to underinvestment and long lead times for new mining projects.

item.title: Aluminum Facing 'Very Large Supply Hole,' JPM Says
item.description: J.P. Morgan analysts predict aluminum prices will rise toward $4,000 per metric ton due to a significant supply shortage exacerbated by Middle East conflicts. The base case suggests prices will stabilize around $3,500 per metric ton in the second half of 2026.

item.title: The Outlook For Aluminum, Steel & Copper Prices
item.description: J.P. Morgan forecasts an average aluminum price of $2,200/mt for Q2 2025, with global demand growth reduced to 1% year-over-year, creating a surplus of 200 kmt in 2025. The outlook reflects adjusted demand estimates and market surplus projections.

item.title: Indonesian smelters cut production on lower nickel ore quota
item.description: Indonesia's energy ministry reduced the 2026 nickel ore production quota to 260–270 million metric tons, below the 320 million tons produced last year and the industry's demand estimate of 340–350 million tons. This cut aims to address previous oversupply and depressed prices, leading Indonesian smelters to reduce their production output.

item.title: US Midwest Domestic Hot-Rolled Coil Steel Futures Historical Data
item.description: Over the past 12 months, US Steel Coil futures have changed by 30.92%, reflecting significant market volatility. Today's trading range for US Steel Coil futures is between 1,153.00 and 1,158.00.

item.title: We just got our revised steel prices for this month. It's bad
item.description: Users report receiving revised steel pricing updates for the month, with prices up another 21.6% since March. The total price increase year-to-date is 58.8%, driven by major spikes in costs during recent months.

item.title: Copper up 9% since Iran war, near January peak. Will AI boom, shortage propel red metal to new highs?
item.description: Copper prices are surging due to geopolitical tensions, structural supply shortages, and booming AI-driven demand. The metal is increasingly viewed as a strategic commodity for the AI era, with markets anticipating a prolonged supply crunch due to underinvestment and long lead times for new mining projects[2].

item.title: Copper prices hit a new record high, but is the metals trade 'stretched'?
item.description: Copper hit a fresh record high, while gold and silver trade near their highs. The metal continues to climb after breaking above $13,000 a ton for the first time[3].

item.title: Copper price falls on Grasberg production resumption
item.description: Freeport McMoRan has restarted copper concentrate supply to a domestic smelter in Indonesia, though the export ban on concentrates from its iconic Grasberg mine remains in place. Reuters reports that staged production resumed following a 38-day hiatus caused by a strike at a domestic refinery where the US company holds a minority stake.

item.title: Development of $9bn copper and gold project pushed back
item.description: A major $9 billion copper and gold project has been delayed as the ongoing Middle East war exacerbates worsening local security conditions. This development adds to capacity constraints in the global copper market amid rising demand.

item.title: Anglo American and Codelco complete Los Bronces - Andina agreement
item.description: Anglo American and Codelco have finalized an agreement for the Los Bronces - Andina project, paving the way to deliver an additional 2.7 million tons of copper production. This capacity increase is expected to help address current supply deficits in the global market.

item.title: Copper producers report higher earnings in Q1 2026
item.description: Copper producers, including Atlas Mining, reported elevated earnings in the first quarter of 2026 driven by high copper prices, though concentrate output remains under pressure. The report highlights ongoing challenges in securing adequate production capacity amid global supply disruptions.

item.title: Record Copper Prices Signal Supply Race
item.description: Analysts at Citi estimate refined copper production will reach 26.9 million tons this year, indicating a deficit of 308,000 tons. Record prices are driving increased investments in new copper capacity to close the supply gap.

item.title: Nickel Prices Off The Bottom
item.description: Nickel prices have broken through US$10,000/t, with a new monthly report by SNL Metals & Mining expressing positive outlook on the market. This marks a significant recovery from previous lows.

item.title: Italy's Prysmian sees stronger U.S. pricing, margins after new copper tariff set-up
item.description: Italy's Prysmian Group is expected to benefit from new U.S. copper tariffs that levy taxes on foreign-made cables, according to the company's CEO. The Italian cable manufacturer anticipates improved pricing and margins in the U.S. market due to these recent tariff adjustments.

item.title: Aluminium Price Today
item.description: The current price of Aluminium futures is 3,193.60, with a previous close of 3,199.00, reflecting minor intraday volatility in the market. The daily trading range shows stability around the 3,199.00 level.

item.title: What the Data Says: Steel Price Updates
item.description: Steel prices have tumbled by 2.85%, continuing a long-term trend where prices have plummeted over 16.5% in recent periods[7].

item.title: Unleashing sustainable nickel production for a net zero future
item.description: This article investigates how optimizing nickel production can create a sustainable global battery industry by implementing technologies that reduce greenhouse gas emissions and improve energy efficiency. It emphasizes that enhancing stakeholder engagement and robust ESG disclosure standards are essential for transparency in the global nickel sector.

item.title: Indonesia's Green Nickel: From Us to the Next Generation
item.description: Neo Energy shares a vision for sustainable industrialization at ICM 2026, focusing on reducing diesel dependency and lowering carbon emissions in nickel mining operations. This initiative supports the broader goal of creating a cleaner mining environment and advancing green nickel production for future generations.

item.title: Nuevo método para la obtención de cobre de forma más sostenible
item.description: Investigadores de la UPM y UNIZAR han desarrollado un método hidrometalúrgico que aumenta la extracción de cobre de minerales ricos en calcopirita hasta un 20% mediante catalizadores de carbono derivados de biomasa. Este proceso también reduce la movilidad del arsénico y la formación de cristales de azufre, disminuyendo el impacto ambiental del método.

item.title: La Universidad de Edimburgo licencia una novedosa tecnología para extraer oro y cobre de residuos electrónicos
item.description: La Universidad de Edimburgo ha concedido a Lithium Universe la licencia para explotar un proceso innovador que permite una extracción más limpia de metales de alto valor, como oro y cobre, a partir de residuos electrónicos. Esta tecnología representa un avance significativo en la recuperación sostenible de metales estratégicos.

item.title: Cobre: un metal fundamental para el desarrollo renovable
item.description: El cobre es un pilar clave de la transición energética global, con proyecciones de demanda que podrían aumentar entre 40% y 60% hacia 2040 en escenarios de descarbonización acelerada. Se recomiendan líneas de acción específicas como la promoción de la exploración responsable, el refuerzo de la cadena de valor industrial y el apoyo a la innovación tecnológica y la economía circular.

item.title: Current Aluminum Prices - US Metal Prices
item.description: RecycleInMe provides daily US metal prices for aluminum, including historic price graphs and current market data for traders and analysts. The platform offers real-time updates on aluminum pricing trends in the US market.

item.title: Aluminum - Price, Chart, Historical Data, News
item.description: Aluminum prices decreased to 3,224.00 USD/T, the lowest since March 2026, with a 12.28% loss over the past four weeks. Despite the recent decline, the commodity has increased 25.28% over the last 12 months.

item.title: Aluminum Price (Monthly)
item.description: The monthly aluminum price is at 3,666.10, up 1.84% from last month and significantly higher than one year ago at 2,448.79. This data reflects the ongoing upward trend in aluminum pricing over the past year.

item.title: Aluminum Market Prices, Data, and Analysis
item.description: S&P Global offers forward-looking intelligence on aluminum prices, market data, and news for energy traders, analysts, and investment professionals. The platform provides comprehensive analysis of non-ferrous metal markets.

item.title: Aluminum Prices and Aluminum Futures Prices
item.description: Barchart provides today's aluminum prices, latest charts, news, and futures quotes for traders monitoring the market. The platform offers real-time updates on aluminum pricing and futures movements.

item.title: Aluminium Market Data
item.description: Fastmarkets delivers prices, news, and analysis of aluminium to help stakeholders keep up with the market's movements. The platform provides comprehensive data on aluminium market trends and pricing.

item.title: Inside Century Aluminum's SC expansion
item.description: FOX Business correspondent Lydia Hu reports from South Carolina on how Century Aluminum is boosting U.S. production thanks to Trump-era policies. The video covers the details of the company's expansion efforts in the region.

item.title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Steel prices rose to 3058 CNY/T on June 30, 2026, marking a 0.07% increase from the previous day. Over the past month, steel prices have fallen 4.14%, though they remain 0.92% higher than the same period last year[3].

item.title: HRC Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Hot-Rolled Coil (HRC) Steel increased to 1194.00 USD/T, reaching its highest level since March 2023. Over the past four weeks, HRC Steel gained 4.83%, showing strong upward momentum in the market[5].

item.title: Steel Price News: Are Prices About to Drop?
item.description: Current steel market prices continue to trend higher across hot-rolled coil, cold-rolled steel, plate, and coated products due to tighter domestic supply. The article examines whether these rising prices might soon reverse[2].

item.title: Exploration at Okiep's Flat Mine East
item.description: Exploration activities at Okiep's Flat Mine East in the Northern Cape are uncovering new copper potential. The region is becoming a significant source of copper, adding to global capacity.

item.title: Nickel Headed to $20,000 as Indonesia Limits Production, EV Demand Surges
item.description: With Indonesia committing to flat 2026 production of 250–260 million tons and EV demand growing 6–7% annually, nickel fundamentals are strengthening to support prices near $20,000 per ton. The market enters 2026 with constructive fundamentals despite lingering analyst skepticism.

item.title: Italy's Prysmian seals $1 bln buy of Channell to grow US presence
item.description: Prysmian has agreed to acquire U.S. connectivity device manufacturer Channell Commercial Corp for up to $1.15 billion to expand its presence in the United States. The deal was announced on Tuesday by the world's largest cable maker.
